trigger.reason: A pre-freeze catalog audit found that Gemma 4 was publicly available before the July 2026 candidate set was finalized. The installed-inventory-first selection had therefore treated Gemma 3 as current without a defensible recency justification.
trigger.scientific_consequence: Pause quality screening and test the current same-size Gemma generation before fixing the finalist set. Preserve all Gemma 3 evidence as historical pilot evidence.

selection_basis: current Gemma generation available before the original candidate set was finalized
same nominal 12B scale as the completed Gemma 3 condition, reducing size confounding
instruction-tuned multimodal Ollama package with an immutable tag rather than latest
selected before pulling the package or inspecting any task output

execution_rule: perform only an additive pull after confirming capacity and current DGX health through $davneet-dgx-access
record the full installed digest and preflight evidence before measured inference
collect exactly one one-shot outcome for each of the same 20 pilot images
if and only if a one-shot invalid outcome ends with done_reason=length at 384 tokens, permit exactly one explicit source-linked 768-token Protocol 2.2 repair
do not repair timeouts, quality failures, or a failed repair
report one-shot and pipeline validity separately
do not inspect comparative metadata quality or select a winner before the amendment is complete and the reviewer package is blinded
advancement_rule: If Gemma 4 meets the unchanged 95% pipeline-validity gate, it supersedes Gemma 3 as the current-generation Gemma challenger in quality screening. Gemma 3 remains reported as historical compatibility evidence. If Gemma 4 fails, retain that failure and document whether Gemma 3 remains only as a legacy-generation comparator or the protocol requires reassessment.
